title: Stabilize Agenda task-lineage resolution behind one shared exact-id policy
goal: Reduce pre-Stage-4 trace-chain risk by making Agenda resolve task-to-deliverable-to-goal lineage through one explicit shared seam instead of mixing implicit reconstruction with transient write-time context.
context: The pre-Stage-4 refactor report identifies task-layer lineage policy as the highest-risk remaining Stage 3 seam because Stage 4 stewardship and later repository readers will depend on truthful task ancestry.
The live Agenda baseline already has exact parent-deliverable validation and goal-level DAG artifacts, but task-to-goal lineage is not yet owned by one explicit shared helper.
Wave D should tighten this seam without inventing a broad shared repository abstraction or beginning Domum behavior.
requirements: Create one small Agenda lineage module that resolves exact task ancestry from task to deliverable to goal through the canonical Formam and Agenda storage surfaces.
Make Agenda build and DAG refresh paths use the shared lineage helper rather than reconstructing goal lineage independently in multiple modules.

Choose and document one explicit live policy for task lineage that preserves the current Stage 3 baseline: tasks remain directly parented by deliverable_id, and goal lineage is resolved through the shared exact-id seam rather than stored as a second canonical parent backlink.
If transient or write-only task fields are no longer needed after the shared lineage seam exists, remove or stop depending on them.
Keep the cleanup limited to lineage truth and validation behavior. Do not add task lifecycle mutation commands, archive behavior, or repository-wide health readers.
acceptance_criteria: Agenda has one obvious shared helper for resolving task ancestry from task to deliverable to goal.
Agenda build, task reads, and DAG refresh no longer reconstruct goal lineage through separate ad hoc logic paths.
The Stage 3 task baseline still uses deliverable_id as the direct parent backlink, and goal lineage is machine-checkable through one explicit shared seam.
suggested_commit_message: stabilize agenda lineage resolution for stage 3 wave d

title: Extract Scintilla storage and exact-id artifact helpers into a focused module
goal: Reduce pre-Stage-4 Scintilla risk by centralizing path ownership, exact spark artifact lookup, and shared read-surface helpers without changing the current CLI surface.
context: The pre-Stage-4 refactor report identifies Scintilla storage and exact-id handling as the other high-risk seam because later archive, review, status, and doctor work will need to reason about spark artifacts truthfully.
Unlike Finis, Formam, and Agenda, Scintilla still spreads raw data/scintilla path decisions across multiple modules and rebuilds the audio-transcript-sparks bundle locally in several places.
Wave D should preserve the current `azq capture`, `azq sparks`, `azq spark <id>`, `azq spark search <text>`, and `azq spark rm <id>` surface while tightening the shared storage seam behind it.
requirements: Create a focused Scintilla storage module that owns canonical data/scintilla path constants, directory helpers, exact spark artifact lookup, and stable spark-file listing.
Route spark list, view, search, and delete behavior through the shared helpers rather than rebuilding the same artifact-path logic in each command module.
Use the shared storage helpers for audio, transcript, and spark output paths where capture, transcription, and extraction already write canonical artifacts.
Keep the current CLI entrypoints and current destructive delete behavior unchanged in this task. This wave should prepare later stewardship work, not replace it.
acceptance_criteria: Scintilla path ownership and exact spark artifact resolution live in one focused module instead of being duplicated across command modules.
The current Scintilla read surface still behaves the same at the CLI level.
A later repository reader can discover the audio, transcript, and spark artifacts for one exact spark id through one shared helper seam.
suggested_commit_message: extract scintilla storage and exact id helpers

title: Align top-level CLI help and high-signal docs to the live Stage 3 surface
goal: Remove operator-facing command and layout drift that would otherwise mislead pre-Stage-4 implementation work, while keeping future-stage ideas clearly separated from live behavior.
context: The pre-Stage-4 refactor report identifies stale command framing and stale layout teaching as a real implementation risk, not just a cosmetic annoyance.
The live code root is `azq/`, and the live Stage 3 operator surface is the narrow `azq agenda ...` family rather than the older broader `azq task ...` or `azq dag ...` framing.
The top-level CLI help in azq/cli.py is also stale enough to teach an older stage boundary and redundant command prefixing.
requirements: Update azq/cli.py help text so it describes the current live command listing accurately and no longer frames itself as a Stage 2 snapshot.
Update README.md and AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md so they teach the current package root, canonical storage locations, and live command surface accurately.
Update AZQ_COMMAND_MODEL.md and AZQ_ENGINE_SPEC.md so they clearly distinguish between the live Stage 1 through Stage 3 surface and later-stage command aspirations.
Update AZQ_FILESYSTEM_MODEL.md and AZQ_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md where they still teach `src/azq/` or other stale layout wording instead of the live repo structure.
Keep the docs grounded in the working repository. Do not use this task to define Stage 4 or Stage 5 behavior beyond clearly labeling it as future work.
acceptance_criteria: The highest-signal docs and top-level CLI help all describe `azq/` as the live package root.
The docs no longer present older `azq task ...`, `azq dag ...`, `azq goal create`, or `azq spark show <id>` syntax as the live operator surface when the code does not expose it.
A new operator can read the updated help and docs and understand the current Stage 1 through Stage 3 baseline without being taught the wrong command family.
suggested_commit_message: align cli help and docs with live stage 3 surface

title: Add a compact Scintilla regression slice for shared read-surface behavior
goal: Protect the Wave D Scintilla cleanup by adding a small seam-level regression suite around the current spark read and exact-id flows, without turning Wave D into a broad Scintilla test campaign.
context: The pre-Stage-4 refactor report identifies missing Scintilla seam coverage as the main test gap still resting on manual trust.
Wave D is a cleanup wave, so the highest-value tests are the ones that prove the shared storage extraction preserved the live read surface.
The repository already uses staged regression files under tests/, so a small Stage 3 Wave D regression file is the natural place to lock this seam.
requirements: Add a compact regression suite for the current Scintilla read surface and exact-id behavior.
Cover stable spark listing, exact-id inspection through the live `azq spark <id>` surface, spark search, and current spark deletion behavior if those commands remain live after the storage cleanup.
Use temporary repository data so the tests exercise the canonical Scintilla artifact layout rather than depending on checked-in live data files.
Run through the repository's standard unittest discovery path instead of requiring a custom entrypoint.
Keep the suite focused on preserved behavior. Do not broaden into microphone capture runtime, Whisper performance, or Stage 4 archive semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Stage 3 Wave D adds one small regression file under tests/ focused on Scintilla seam behavior.
The shared Scintilla storage extraction is protected by tests at the live command or public helper surface rather than by internal implementation details alone.
The repository's standard unittest discovery run can include the new Wave D regression slice successfully.
suggested_commit_message: add stage 3 wave d scintilla regression coverage
